details: Massive concentration of chlorophyll detected at the ocean surface surface.
New clutch of larvae hatched safely near an anemone host.
Water temperature rose 1.5°C above seasonal average in 2 hours.
Polyp retraction and mucus production observed due to heat stress.
Dense bait ball formed to graze on the plankton bloom.
Solitary apex predator detected patrolling the edge of the reef.
Large school of predators attacking the anchovy bait ball.
Visible pigment loss across 15% of the local colony.
Cool, nitrogen-rich water rising from the shelf break.
Juvenile shrimp emerging in the nutrient-rich nursery grounds.
Vertical migration toward mid-depths beginning as sunlight fades.
Ambush attack on a small damselfish during evening transition.
